content:
# Paleontology, General

**Summary**: The study of prehistoric life before adoption of modern biological terminology.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2006-al-enezi|Al-Enezi, 2006]] applied paleontology to analyze foraminiferal assemblages.

- [[2016-malik|Malik, 2016]] conducted micropaleontological analysis on the Dhruma Formation.

- [[2017-amao|Amao, 2017]] applied paleontology to describe new species and analyze foraminiferal assemblages in the Arabian Gulf.

- [[2022-khamsin|Khamsin, 2022]] focused on paleontology to analyze foraminifera and nannofossils.

- [[2024-hikmahtiar|Hikmahtiar, 2024]] used paleontology to reconstruct the impact of mass extinction on marine biota.

- [[2024-alhilali|Alhilali, 2024]] applied paleontology to analyze the microfossil assemblages from IODP Site U1512.

## Key Subtopics

- [[2006-al-enezi|Al-Enezi, 2006]] ['foraminifera', 'microfossils', 'paleoecology']

- [[2016-malik|Malik, 2016]] conducted micropaleontological analysis on the Dhruma Formation.

- [[2017-amao|Amao, 2017]] applied paleontology to describe new species and analyze foraminiferal assemblages in the Arabian Gulf.

- [[2022-khamsin|Khamsin, 2022]] focused on paleontology to analyze foraminifera and nannofossils.

- [[2024-hikmahtiar|Hikmahtiar, 2024]] used paleontology to reconstruct the impact of mass extinction on marine biota.

- [[2024-alhilali|Alhilali, 2024]] applied paleontology to analyze the microfossil assemblages from IODP Site U1512.

## Methods and Approaches Used

- [[2006-al-enezi|Al-Enezi, 2006]] ['thin-section-petrography']

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Identified 47 species from the Arabian Gulf and various benthic foraminifera in the Dam Formation. ([[2006-al-enezi|Al-Enezi, 2006, p. 59]])

- Numerous foraminifera species were identified, including those from genera such as Siphovalvulina and Riyadhella. ([[2016-malik|Malik, 2016, p. 70]])

- A new genus, Pseudonubeculina, was established based on unique morphological features like a slit-like aperture. ([[2017-amao|Amao, 2017, p. 40]])

- 8209 benthic foraminiferal specimens were analyzed across 50 samples. ([[2022-khamsin|Khamsin, 2022, p. 38]])

- Identified three groups of DWAF: survivor species, extinction species, and incoming species. ([[2024-hikmahtiar|Hikmahtiar, 2024, p. 16]])

- Analysis revealed a diverse assemblage of planktonic, calcareous benthic, and agglutinated foraminifera. ([[2024-alhilali|Alhilali, 2024, p. 18]])

## Competing Approaches and Debates

- [[2006-al-enezi|Al-Enezi, 2006]] Distinguishing between modern and fossil assemblages in similar environments.
